content.problemStatement:
Chef has fallen in love with Cheffina, and wants to buy NNN gifts for her. On reaching the gift shop, Chef got to know the following two things:


The cost of each gift is 111 coin.
On the purchase of every 4th4^{th}4th gift, Chef gets the 5th5^{th}5th gift free of cost.
What is the minimum number of coins that Chef will require in order to come out of the shop carrying NNN gifts?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT — the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer NNN, the number of gifts in the shop.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of coins that Chef will require to obtain all NNN gifts.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the number of gifts Chef wants.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of coins Chef has to spend to obtain N gifts.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=5, one gift is free (5/5=1), so Chef pays 5-1=4 coins. For N=4, no free gift, so he pays 4 coins.
N=1 → 1-0=1. N=10 → 10-2=8 (two free gifts). N=23 → 23-4=19 (four free gifts).

solution.approach: For every group of 5 gifts Chef pays for only 4 because the 5th is free. Hence the number of free gifts equals floor(N/5). The minimum coins required = N - floor(N/5). Compute this directly for each test case using integer division.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
it = iter(data)
T = int(next(it))
out = []
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(next(it))
    out.append(str(N - N//5))
sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
